title: [$250] Add Custom Field 1, Custom Field 2, and International Reimbursement IDs columns

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Summary Add Report Created, Custom Field 1, Custom Field 2, and Reimbursement IDs columns to match what's available in the detailed export template and close the gap between in-product column configuration and exports. This is part of a larger effort to add custom columns: - https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/622813 ## Expected Behavior References to the template below are included here. - Users can display the Custom Field 1 column (custom field data): `${report.submitterUserID}` - Users can display the Custom Field 2 column (custom field data): `${report.submitterPayrollID}` - Users can display the International Reimbursement IDs column (associated reimbursement identifiers): `${report.orderDealNumbers}` - Columns follow existing Expensify column styling and behavior ## Context Currently, ~29 columns are available in-product; the detailed export template has 67. Top customers have requested the ability to configure columns in the product that match their export templates to reduce downstream Excel manipulation. Reference: `Web-Expensify/lib/Integrations/defaultExportTemplates/detailed_export.ftl` ([expensify_detailed_export.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27710929/expensify_detailed_export.txt)) for context on available columns. ## Performance Considerations - Verify no performance impact on accounts with large report histories - Monitor render times when displaying these columns alongside other data ## Testing - Verify columns display correctly with various data states - Verify columns are configurable via the column selection menu - Test on accounts with large report histories for performance impact
Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022054555679057728780
  • Upwork Job ID: 2054555679057728780
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-05-13

body:
## Summary Add First Approver and First Approved date columns to the reports columns to match what's available in the detailed export template and close the gap between in-product column configuration and exports. This is part of a larger effort to add custom columns: - https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/622813 ## Expected Behavior References to the template below are included here. - Users can display the First Approver column (person who approved the report): `${report.approvers[0].email}` - Users can display the First Approved column (timestamp of first approval): `${report.approvers[0].date?date("yyyy-MM-dd")?string("yyyy-MM-dd")}` ## Context Currently, ~29 columns are available in-product; the detailed export template has 67. Top customers have requested the ability to configure columns in the product that match their export templates to reduce downstream Excel manipulation. Reference: `Web-Expensify/lib/Integrations/defaultExportTemplates/detailed_export.ftl` ([expensify_detailed_export.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27710929/expensify_detailed_export.txt)) for context on available columns. ## Performance Considerations - Verify no performance impact on accounts with large report histories - Monitor render times when displaying these columns alongside other data ## Testing - Verify columns display correctly with various data states - Verify columns are configurable via the column selection menu - Test on accounts with large report histories for performance impact

title: [$250] Add MCC, Tax Code and Category GL code columns for Expenses

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Summary Add MCC (Merchant Category Code) and Tax Code columns to match what's available in the detailed export template and close the gap between in-product column configuration and exports. This is part of a larger effort to add custom columns: - https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/622813 ## Expected Behavior References to the template below are included here. - Users can display the MCC column in their reports: `${expense.mcc}` - Users can display the Tax Code column in their reports: `${txCode}` (simply logic in template below) - Users can display the Category GL column in their reports: `${expense.categoryGlCode?replace("\"", "")}` - Columns follow existing Expensify column styling and behavior ## Context Currently, ~29 columns are available in-product; the detailed export template has 67. Top customers have requested the ability to configure columns in the product that match their export templates to reduce downstream Excel manipulation. Reference: `Web-Expensify/lib/Integrations/defaultExportTemplates/detailed_export.ftl` ([expensify_detailed_export.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27710929/expensify_detailed_export.txt)) for context on available columns. ## Performance Considerations - Verify no performance impact on accounts with large report histories - Monitor render times when displaying these columns alongside other data ## Testing - Verify columns display correctly with various data states - Verify columns are configurable via the column selection menu - Test on accounts with large report histories for performance impact

title: [$250] [iOS] Bottom tab bar overlaps composer when returning to app on Report screen

labels.name: Reviewing

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
On native iOS, if you background the app while on a Report screen and then return to it, the bottom tab bar renders on top of the composer input, making it impossible to type. Navigating away and back to the report fixes it. **Steps to reproduce:** 1. Open a report/chat 2. Background the app 3. Return to the app **Expected:** Composer is accessible as normal **Actual:** Bottom tab bar overlaps the composer

title: [$250] Migrate InitialListValueSelectorModal to @react-navigation modal screen

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Context This is a sub-issue of #53493 (Replace modal screens with modals from `@react-navigation`). For full background and migration approach, see the [triage analysis comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/53493#issuecomment-4312826488) and the [sub-issue breakdown comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/53493#issuecomment-4439641260). ## Problem `InitialListValueSelectorModal` is currently implemented using `react-native-modal`, which causes animation inconsistencies on native platforms. ## Migration approach (Group 3 — Write to known form draft) Used via `InputWrapper` in only **one place** with a known `formID`/`inputID`. The new navigation page writes directly to the Onyx form draft, and `FormProvider` merges it reactively. - **File:** `src/pages/workspace/reports/InitialListValueSelector/InitialListValueSelectorModal.tsx` - **Used by:** Only `InitialListValueSelector` → `CreateReportFieldsPage.tsx` - **On select:** `updateValueInput(initialValue)` → `onInputChange(value === initialValue ? '' : initialValue)` (toggle behavior) - **State destination:** Form `WORKSPACE_REPORT_FIELDS_FORM`, field `initialValue` ### Steps 1. Create a new `@react-navigation` modal route for initial list value selection. 2. Extract the modal content into a standalone screen component. 3. On selection, write directly to `WORKSPACE_REPORT_FIELDS_FORM` form draft field `initialValue` via Onyx (preserving the toggle behavior: if selected value equals current value, write empty string), then navigate back. 4. Replace the `` usage with a navigation call. 5. Test the report fields creation flow on iOS, Android, and web. ## Reference - Reference PR for this migration pattern: https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/90346 - Parent tracking issue: #53493

title: [$250] Migrate YearPickerModal to @react-navigation modal screen

labels.name: External

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Context This is a sub-issue of #53493 (Replace modal screens with modals from `@react-navigation`). For full background and migration approach, see the [triage analysis comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/53493#issuecomment-4312826488) and the [sub-issue breakdown comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/53493#issuecomment-4439641260). ## Problem `YearPickerModal` animates in from right-to-left like a regular screen, but uses `react-native-modal` internally. This causes inconsistent animations compared to standard `@react-navigation` screens on native. ## Migration approach (Group 2 — Lift local `useState` to Onyx) - **File:** `src/components/DatePicker/CalendarPicker/YearPickerModal.tsx` - **Used by:** Only `CalendarPicker/index.tsx` - **On select:** `onYearChange(option.value)` → updates `currentDateView` state in `CalendarPicker` - **State to lift:** The `currentDateView` year component from `CalendarPicker` → a shared Onyx key. - **Note:** `CalendarPicker` is embedded in `DatePickerModal` and `ScheduleCallPage` and owns complex month/year/day state, making this the trickiest in Group 2. ### Steps 1. Lift the selected year state from `CalendarPicker` to an Onyx key. 2. Create a new `@react-navigation` modal route for year selection. 3. Extract `YearPickerModal` content into a standalone screen component that writes the selected year to Onyx. 4. Update `CalendarPicker` to read from Onyx instead of local state for the year. 5. Test all flows that use `DatePicker` (date of birth, IOURequestStepDate, SetDatePage, etc.) on iOS, Android, and web. ## Reference - Reference PR for this migration pattern: https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88915 - Parent tracking issue: #53493

labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
@dukenv0307 cc @justinpersaud After PR #89454, SmartScan failure messages include the missing fields (e.g., Receipt canning failed — missing merchant, date, and amount). This new field-specific message missing fields (e.g., Receipt scanning failed — missing merchant, date, and amount) displays correctly in the concierge chat and the receipt error pill, but two display surfaces still show the generic Receipt scanning failed message: Report name and LHN last message preview Repro steps: 1. Upload a non-receipt image as an expense (so SmartScan fails with missing merchant/date/amount). 2. Wait for the violation to land. 3. Open a chat that has only one expense — observe that the report name shows Receipt scanning failed. (expected: Receipt scanning failed — missing merchant, date, and amount). 4. Look at the LHN sidebar preview for that chat — same generic message (expected: field-specific).
